phenotypes.label: Metabolic syndrome X
phenotypes.description: Anchored by the diagnosis of metabolic syndrome (metabolic syndrome X), a cluster of central obesity, hypertension, dyslipidemia, and insulin resistance. Patients often report fatigue and have related comorbidities such as obstructive sleep apnea and musculoskeletal pain; other symptoms reflect general comorbidity rather than a distinct second theme.

phenotypes.label: Aneurysm and aortic ectasia
phenotypes.description: Records centered on aneurysm diagnoses, often reflecting aortic aneurysm or thoracic aortic ectasia. Frequently accompanied by cardiovascular risk factors (hypertension, hyperlipidemia) and age‑associated comorbidities such as GERD, dysphagia, diverticular disease, and incidental lung nodules.

phenotypes.label: Benign prostatic hyperplasia with LUTS
phenotypes.description: Men with benign prostatic hyperplasia and bladder outlet obstruction presenting with lower urinary tract symptoms such as nocturia, urinary frequency, and retention/obstruction. Elevated prostate-specific antigen is common, with associated hypogonadism and erectile dysfunction; inguinal hernia appears as a frequent comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: GERD with esophagitis
phenotypes.description: Patients present primarily with gastroesophageal reflux disease with esophagitis, reflecting erosive reflux and esophageal inflammation. Coexisting conditions such as hepatic steatosis, pulmonary nodules or abnormal lung imaging, and cervical spine pain are frequently documented but are not the defining feature.

phenotypes.label: Melanoma with pulmonary imaging abnormalities
phenotypes.description: Malignant melanoma co-occurs with pulmonary abnormalities, including abnormal lung fields and solitary pulmonary nodules. The presence of primary kidney cancer and pancreatic/liver disorders indicates a solid-tumor oncology profile with pulmonary involvement and staging/surveillance findings on lung imaging.

phenotypes.label: Autoimmune hypothyroidism with B12 deficiency
phenotypes.description: Autoimmune thyroid disease is prominent, with acquired hypothyroidism, thyroid atrophy/goiter, and fatigue. Co-occurring vitamin B12 and vitamin D deficiencies with megaloblastic anemia, plus osteoporosis in postmenopausal women, suggest nutritional and bone health complications within an autoimmune milieu. Raynaud phenomenon and impaired fasting glycemia appear in a subset, reflecting broader autoimmune clustering and metabolic comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: PTSD, depression, anxiety, substance dependence
phenotypes.description: Trauma- and mood-related psychiatric conditions cluster with alcohol and other substance use disorders. PTSD, recurrent major depression, generalized anxiety/panic, and insomnia co-occur with alcohol, opioid, cocaine, cannabis, and nicotine dependence, with suicidality and personality disorders; dental disease appears as a comorbidity often seen in substance use.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix generic chronic comorbidities such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and obesity with scattered, unrelated diagnoses. There is no coherent clinical pattern tying these together, so this grouping is not useful for phenotype identification.

phenotypes.label: ADHD and childhood emotional disorders
phenotypes.description: Predominantly inattentive ADHD co-occurs with childhood emotional and behavioral disorders, with additional mood and anxiety spectrum diagnoses such as dysthymia, depressive disorder, generalized anxiety, and occasional bipolar disorder. This reflects a pediatric neurodevelopmental and emotional comorbidity cluster.

phenotypes.label: Degenerative lumbar spine with radiculopathy
phenotypes.description: Degenerative spine disease centered on the lumbar region with radiculopathy/sciatica and neurogenic claudication. Findings include lumbar disc degeneration, spondylosis/spondylolisthesis, and spinal stenosis with associated chronic low back and neck pain, sacroiliac and sacral disorders, and regional joint pains. Chronic pain syndromes and fibromyalgia co-occur, reflecting persistent multisite musculoskeletal pain.

phenotypes.label: Functional bloating and arterial disease
phenotypes.description: Two unrelated clusters are present. One centers on gastrointestinal gas symptoms — abdominal distension, flatulence, stool abnormalities, IBS‑type complaints, and mild constipation/gastritis. The other reflects arterial/cerebrovascular disease with neurologic features (generic arterial disorder, carotid obstruction, slurred speech, dysarthria, amnesia), so this is a merged set of themes rather than a single phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Valvular disease with connective tissue features
phenotypes.description: Cluster of nonrheumatic valvular heart disease centered on mitral valve prolapse and aortic regurgitation, with heart murmur, palpitations, dyspnea, cardiomegaly, and pulmonary hypertension. Co-occurrence with scoliosis and other bone/joint findings suggests an underlying connective tissue phenotype with cardiovascular involvement.

phenotypes.label: Kidney stones and ocular degeneration
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between urinary stone disease (kidney stones with hydronephrosis and microscopic hematuria) and degenerative ocular disorders (nonexudative macular degeneration, cataract, vitreous degeneration, refractive errors). These reflect unrelated organ systems without a unifying mechanism, indicating merged themes rather than a single phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Ischemic stroke and TIA
phenotypes.description: Ischemic cerebrovascular events dominate, particularly cerebral infarction due to arterial thrombosis and transient ischemic attack. Associated focal neurologic deficits include dysarthria, slurred speech, ataxia, dysphagia, and amnesia, consistent with acute and subacute stroke presentations.

phenotypes.label: Hip region enthesopathy
phenotypes.description: Defined by enthesopathy of the hip region—disorders of tendon/ligament insertions around the greater trochanter or pelvis—typically presenting with lateral hip pain and tenderness. Other co-occurring conditions are heterogeneous and do not drive the pattern.

phenotypes.label: Female breast cancer and sequelae
phenotypes.description: Female breast malignancy with associated breast findings is prominent, including abnormal mammography, palpable breast lump, quadrant‑specific primary tumors, and benign breast conditions. Genetic predisposition, postoperative state, and lymphedema appear alongside breast pain; bone and articular disorders and osteoporosis are also represented.

phenotypes.label: Varicose veins and venous insufficiency
phenotypes.description: Chronic lower-extremity venous disease with varicose veins and peripheral venous insufficiency, presenting with leg swelling/edema and lymphedema. Associated features include lower-limb pain, cellulitis, peripheral vascular disease, and occasional acute deep vein thrombosis or chronic leg ulceration.

phenotypes.label: Glaucoma and age-related ocular disease
phenotypes.description: Cluster of ophthalmic conditions centered on primary open-angle glaucoma and ocular hypertension with glaucoma suspect codes, alongside age-related cataracts. Co-occurring ocular surface and posterior segment findings (tear film insufficiency/dry eye, blepharitis, vitreous degeneration, epiretinal membrane) and visual disturbance or refractive errors indicate degenerative eye disease with vision impairment risk. Systemic hypertension and hyperlipidemia appear as background comorbidities.

phenotypes.label: Pregnancy and obstetric complications
phenotypes.description: Pregnancy-related conditions dominate, including coding for all trimesters, high‑risk and complicated pregnancies, and early gestation. Associated findings include amenorrhea, pelvic and lower abdominal pain, genitourinary infections and bleeding, genital herpes and candidiasis, and anemia, with obesity appearing as a frequent comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Epilepsy and parkinsonian movement disorders
phenotypes.description: Patients show epilepsy/seizures alongside parkinsonian movement disorders. Associated findings include tremor, abnormal involuntary movements, dysarthria, cognitive impairment and amnesia, with mood symptoms and autonomic features such as orthostatic hypotension and constipation also represented.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are common baseline comorbidities (hypertension, type 2 diabetes, GERD, obesity) alongside nonspecific pain and gastrointestinal symptoms. Additional items are scattered and unrelated, with no unifying clinical theme to define a specific condition pattern.

phenotypes.label: Bipolar with parathyroid and thyroid disease
phenotypes.description: Bipolar disorder co-occurs with endocrine disease, prominently hyperparathyroidism with hypercalcemia and thyroid nodular goiter, with additional cholelithiasis. Bone disorders/osteoporosis and cardiac arrhythmias align with calcium/parathyroid involvement, while other skin and gynecologic findings appear as secondary comorbidities.

phenotypes.label: Substance use and thoracic emergencies
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between severe mental illness and substance-related crises (suicidal/homicidal ideation, hallucinations, schizophrenia, opioid/cocaine abuse) and acute thoracic pathology (pneumothorax, pleural effusion, pulmonary edema, atelectasis) with chest pain and tachycardia. These reflect disparate clinical pathways with no shared clinical theme across the leading conditions.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral neuropathy and mononeuritis
phenotypes.description: Peripheral nerve disorders dominate, including mononeuritis, polyneuropathy, and neuropathy, with limb pain, altered sensation, and entrapment neuropathies such as carpal tunnel syndrome. Thyroid disease (including multinodular goiter) and monoclonal gammopathy appear and are recognized associations with peripheral neuropathy. Coexisting arthropathy, carotid and coronary atherosclerosis, heart murmur, GERD/hiatal hernia, and cataract suggest an older multimorbid population.

phenotypes.label: Unused low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are a generic mix of common chronic comorbidities and acute symptoms (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, pain, mood symptoms) without a shared mechanism. Additional conditions span unrelated dermatologic, gastrointestinal, musculoskeletal, and administrative findings, reinforcing the lack of a coherent clinical theme.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are very common baseline comorbidities (hypertension, GERD, hyperlipidemia, back pain) mixed with scattered unrelated diagnoses, without a unifying clinical theme. This reads as general background rather than a coherent clinical cluster and is not useful for phenotype selection.

phenotypes.label: Erectile dysfunction
phenotypes.description: Erectile dysfunction dominates, with many patients also carrying hypogonadism and age-related or diabetic eye disorders (presbyopia, hypermetropia, glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy). Obesity and lipid disorders appear alongside these conditions, consistent with cardiometabolic comorbidity in older men.

phenotypes.label: Retinal detachment, glaucoma, diabetic retinopathy
phenotypes.description: Advanced ocular disease centered on retinal detachment, glaucoma, bilateral blindness, and diabetic retinopathy. Cataract, epiretinal membrane, vitreous opacities, myopia, and dry eye co-occur, with diabetes commonly contributing to microvascular retinal damage. This pattern reflects severe retinal pathology and vision loss in a subset of patients.

phenotypes.label: Complicated urinary tract infection and sepsis
phenotypes.description: Urinary tract infection and cystitis with dysuria and hematuria predominate. Bladder dysfunction with urinary retention and hydronephrosis suggests obstructive or neurogenic etiologies, with some patients progressing to bacteremia, sepsis, and acute kidney injury; kidney stones may contribute.

phenotypes.label: Malignancy with postoperative effusions and obstruction
phenotypes.description: Patients with malignancy, often post-resection, showing complications and postoperative sequelae. Prominent features include pleural and pericardial effusions, atelectasis, lung nodules, hepatomegaly, bowel obstruction, and hydronephrosis, consistent with metastatic or postsurgical obstructive processes. This reflects active or prior cancer with thoracoabdominal complications.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split across unrelated systems: dyslipidemia and coagulation disorders; abdominal wall hernias with intra-abdominal masses and postsurgical states; renal malignancy/cysts; and audiologic symptoms (tinnitus, sensorineural hearing loss). Palpitations, flushing, anemia, and hemorrhoids add further heterogeneity. There is no single clinical pathway uniting these findings.

phenotypes.label: Diabetic coronary disease with heart failure
phenotypes.description: Cardiometabolic profile with hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and type 2 diabetes complicated by coronary atherosclerosis, atrial fibrillation, and heart failure (systolic and diastolic). Diabetic kidney disease, obesity with obstructive sleep apnea, dyspnea, and other diabetic complications are common, indicating advanced cardiovascular disease in diabetes.

phenotypes.label: Cirrhosis with portal hypertension complications
phenotypes.description: Advanced chronic liver disease with cirrhosis is prominent, featuring ascites, splenomegaly, thrombocytopenia/pancytopenia, coagulopathy, and abnormal liver tests. Etiologies include chronic hepatitis C and nonalcoholic steatohepatitis, with some patients having a history of liver transplantation.

phenotypes.label: Dysphonia
phenotypes.description: Voice disorder with prominent dysphonia (hoarseness) predominates. Associated conditions include dysphagia and gastroesophageal reflux, with some post‑procedural complication codes, consistent with laryngeal or upper aerodigestive involvement.

phenotypes.label: Rheumatoid arthritis with VTE and osteoporosis
phenotypes.description: Rheumatoid arthritis, largely seropositive, with extra‑articular autoimmune features (Sjögren’s/dry eyes, oral ulcers) and skeletal comorbidity including osteoporosis. There is a strong signal for venous thromboembolism, with deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism (including infarction). Overall this reflects chronic inflammatory arthritis with thromboembolic complications and systemic manifestations.

phenotypes.label: Multiple sclerosis and ADHD
phenotypes.description: Neurologic phenotype dominated by multiple sclerosis and attention‑deficit/hyperactivity disorder, with associated neurologic symptoms such as ataxia, abnormal involuntary movements, sensory disturbances, fatigue, and hypersomnia. Mood and anxiety disorders, chronic pain, restless legs, and headache frequently co-occur, reflecting substantial neuropsychiatric comorbidity in this group.

phenotypes.label: Foot-ankle pain and deformities
phenotypes.description: Musculoskeletal complaints centered on the foot and ankle, including arthralgia, foot pain, plantar fascial disease, flatfoot, ankle sprain, and localized osteoarthritis, with frequent forefoot deformities such as hallux valgus/bunion and hammer toe. Hand and wrist overuse/degenerative conditions (carpal tunnel syndrome, trigger finger, thumb carpometacarpal osteoarthritis) co-occur, reflecting peripheral joint and enthesopathic disorders.

phenotypes.label: HIV with herpesvirus co-infections
phenotypes.description: Patients with human immunodeficiency virus infection, often documented as asymptomatic, alongside concentrated herpesvirus diagnoses such as herpes zoster. Associated findings include seborrheic dermatitis and leukopenia, consistent with immune dysregulation and viral reactivation.

phenotypes.label: Pancytopenia with mucocutaneous bleeding
phenotypes.description: Patients show pancytopenia and thrombocytopenic disorders with prominent epistaxis and other bleeding risk. Associated features include fever, diarrhea, skin eruptions, and immune dysfunction, indicating infection susceptibility alongside cytopenia‑related mucocutaneous bleeding.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic chronic comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, chest or abdominal pain) with scattered outliers like history of kidney transplant. There is no unifying clinical pattern, so this grouping is not clinically interpretable.

phenotypes.label: Hydrocephalus with neurologic symptoms
phenotypes.description: Neurologic presentation centered on hydrocephalus and intracranial pathology. Headache, nausea/vomiting, seizures, dysarthria, reduced mobility, unsteady gait/falls, and nystagmus indicate raised intracranial pressure and focal brain deficits. Postoperative state codes are frequent, consistent with patients recovering from structural brain lesions.

phenotypes.label: Bronchiectasis with pancreatic disease and diabetes
phenotypes.description: Pattern centers on bronchiectasis with dyspnea, cough, hypoxemia, hemoptysis, recurrent pneumonias, and abnormal lung imaging, sometimes with oxygen dependence. It co-occurs with pancreatic disease (chronic/acute pancreatitis) and secondary, pancreatogenic diabetes. This multisystem constellation suggests a cystic fibrosis/CFTR-related disorder in adults.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions mix common chronic comorbidities (hypertension, hyperlipidemia, obesity, anxiety/depression) with nonspecific gastrointestinal complaints (GERD, gastritis, epigastric pain, nausea). There is no shared clinical theme, reading as a general baseline rather than a distinct condition pattern.

phenotypes.label: HIV with chronic obstructive lung disease
phenotypes.description: Patients with HIV infection alongside chronic obstructive lung disease. Comorbid features include immunodeficiency diagnoses, chronic hepatitis C, asthma, and cocaine use with serious mental illness, reflecting HIV-associated pulmonary disease in a high-risk population.

phenotypes.label: Recurrent depression with generalized anxiety
phenotypes.description: Recurrent major depressive disorder is prominent, with codes spanning mild to moderate episodes and multiple remission states, accompanied by generalized anxiety and related anxiety-spectrum diagnoses. Presentations may include nonspecific somatic symptoms such as fatigue. This reflects mood–anxiety comorbidity documented across episodes over time.

phenotypes.label: Ulcerative colitis with GI bleeding
phenotypes.description: Ulcerative colitis and related colitides with prominent lower gastrointestinal bleeding and diarrhea. Recurrent rectal bleeding, melena/hematochezia, and iron deficiency anemia from blood loss suggest active inflammatory bowel disease with chronic or acute hemorrhage.

phenotypes.label: Postprocedural complications and sequelae
phenotypes.description: Patients with documentation of procedure-related complications and postoperative or postprocedural states, including late effects. Prominent associated findings are abnormal radiology results (often lung field abnormalities), lymphadenopathy, abdominal/left lower quadrant pain, intestinal obstruction, syncope, and hypokalemia, consistent with perioperative and postoperative complication patterns.

phenotypes.label: Knee osteoarthritis and pain
phenotypes.description: Degenerative knee osteoarthritis predominates, with related knee pain, effusion, and meniscal tears. Presentations include lower-limb joint pain and swelling; obesity and hypertension appear as common comorbidities in this group.

phenotypes.label: Crohn's disease with abscess
phenotypes.description: Inflammatory bowel disease, predominantly Crohn’s, with anorectal and intra‑abdominal abscesses, perianal skin tags, fecal incontinence, and episodes of bowel obstruction. Presentations include abdominal pain, diarrhea, nausea/vomiting, and reflux or esophagitis, with frequent iron‑deficiency anemia. Some patients are noted to be immunosuppressed.

phenotypes.label: Dysarthria
phenotypes.description: Speech articulation impairment is the central feature, frequently accompanied by dysphagia, nystagmus, diplopia, tremor, and unsteady stance—suggesting a bulbar/brainstem or cerebellar pattern of neurologic involvement. Asthenia and dyspnea are also noted, with common cardiometabolic comorbidities present in the background.

phenotypes.label: Benign skin lesions and photodamage
phenotypes.description: Predominantly cutaneous lesions with features of photodamage: actinic and seborrheic keratoses, melanocytic nevi (including trunk), epidermoid cysts, hemangiomas, warts, and pigmentary changes. Chronic sun/UV exposure is implied, with basal cell carcinoma and rosacea appearing in the same sun‑exposed, older population.

phenotypes.label: Hyperthyroidism and thyroid disorders
phenotypes.description: Pattern centers on thyroid disease, dominated by thyrotoxicosis with nodular goiter and some acquired hypothyroidism. Associated findings include amenorrhea, cardiovascular symptoms, hyperhidrosis, neuropsychiatric complaints (depression, bipolar features, amnestic symptoms), migraine, and dermatologic complaints (rosacea, urticaria), reflecting systemic effects of thyroid dysfunction.

phenotypes.label: Mixed shoulder and oncologic/GI
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between shoulder pain and degenerative shoulder pathology (rotator cuff tear, impingement, bursitis, shoulder osteoarthritis) alongside chronic and postoperative pain terms. At the same time, it emphasizes unrelated gastrointestinal and thoracic conditions such as Crohn’s disease, intestinal obstruction, and primary malignancies of lung, kidney, and colon with surgical complication states. These reflect disparate organ systems without a single unifying clinical mechanism, so this is not a single phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Schizophrenia and schizoaffective spectrum
phenotypes.description: Severe psychotic disorders centered on schizophrenia and schizoaffective disorder, with related psychotic-spectrum diagnoses. Associated features include abnormal involuntary movements, cognitive/organic mental symptoms, dizziness, and sensory complaints such as hearing loss, tinnitus, and visual disturbance. Cardiometabolic comorbidities like hypertension, hyperlipidemia, metabolic syndrome, and diabetes are frequent.

phenotypes.label: Ocular disorders and sexual activity status
phenotypes.description: This combines documentation of sexual activity status with a cluster of ophthalmic disorders (vitreous opacities/degeneration, retinal disease, cataract, glaucoma suspect, dry eye, blepharitis) and sarcoidosis, which can involve the eyes. These represent unrelated themes—social history and eye disease—so this does not form a single coherent clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Fibrocystic breast disease
phenotypes.description: Encounters centered on fibrocystic disease of the breast, often accompanied by abnormal mammography and occasional breast cancer diagnoses. Gynecologic conditions such as ovarian cysts and menstrual bleeding findings commonly co-occur, with assorted general comorbidities appearing variably.

phenotypes.label: Dementia
phenotypes.description: Primary neurocognitive disorder consistent with dementia. Cognitive features (amnesia, attentional deficits) and neuropsychiatric comorbidities (bipolar disorder, anxiety) are common, with occasional Parkinsonism and tremor. Co-listed conditions such as postmenopausal state, osteoarthritis, pneumonia, hypotension, and palpitations reflect comorbidity and frailty in older adults.

phenotypes.label: Obstructive sleep apnea
phenotypes.description: This cluster centers on obstructive sleep apnea, with frequent accompanying headache/migraine, fatigue, and occasional bradycardia. ENT infections (acute pharyngitis) and concurrent musculoskeletal complaints such as back pain and lumbar disc degeneration also appear in this group.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid cancer and post-thyroidectomy sequelae
phenotypes.description: Clinical pattern of thyroid carcinoma and nodular thyroid disease with regional lymphadenopathy, accompanied by postoperative hypothyroidism and hypocalcemia consistent with thyroidectomy sequelae. Dysphonia and dysphagia likely reflect laryngeal or neck surgery effects, and occasional lung nodules or abnormal chest imaging suggest metastatic evaluation or surveillance.

phenotypes.label: Menstrual disorders, vaginitis, and anxiety
phenotypes.description: Patients present with gynecologic and menstrual conditions including abnormal uterine bleeding, irregular menses, vaginitis (including candidal forms), uterine fibroids, endometriosis, ovarian cysts, and PCOS. Mood and headache comorbidities (anxiety, depression, migraine) are prominent, with occasional common upper respiratory infections appearing as concurrent primary care issues.

phenotypes.label: Postmenopausal gyn, hearing loss, polyps
phenotypes.description: Postmenopausal women presenting with menopausal syndrome, postmenopausal bleeding, dyspareunia, and occasional breast lumps. Co-occurring age-related conditions include sensorineural hearing loss with tinnitus and colorectal polyps, with some ENT findings such as impacted cerumen.

phenotypes.label: Mixed dermatologic and mood disorders
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between dermatologic acne/follicular disorders with allergic diathesis and upper airway issues, and a separate cluster of mood and anxiety disorders centered on bipolar II with associated depression and sleep disturbance. These reflect two unrelated clinical themes combined in one group, without a single unifying condition.

phenotypes.label: Allergic rhinitis with asthma and sinusitis
phenotypes.description: Atopic airway disease characterized by allergic rhinitis (often pollen-related), chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal congestion/rhinorrhea, and structural nasal issues such as deviated septum. Lower airway involvement includes asthma across severities, with cough and dyspnea. This reflects an allergic upper–lower airway continuum.

phenotypes.label: Female urinary incontinence and overactive bladder
phenotypes.description: Female urinary incontinence syndromes dominate, including stress, urge, and mixed types, with overactive bladder features such as urgency, frequency, and nocturia. Co-occurring postmenopausal genitourinary changes (atrophic vaginitis) and pelvic floor symptoms (dyspareunia, pelvic pain) are present, with occasional fecal incontinence, consistent with urogynecologic pelvic floor dysfunction.

phenotypes.label: Kidney transplant with CKD complications
phenotypes.description: Chronic kidney disease across stages, including end-stage renal disease and kidney transplant recipients, dominated by hypertensive nephropathy. Complications cluster with anemia of CKD, secondary hyperparathyroidism and phosphorus abnormalities, proteinuria, hyperkalemia, and intermittent acute kidney injury. Immunodeficiency/immunosuppression and postoperative states reflect solid-organ transplantation and its long-term sequelae.

phenotypes.label: Obstructive sleep apnea and hypersomnia
phenotypes.description: Obstructive sleep apnea and other sleep–wake disorders dominate, including hypersomnia, dyssomnia/insomnia, and restless legs. Cognitive and attentional complaints and migraines (including status migrainosus) co-occur, consistent with daytime sleepiness, headache, and neurocognitive effects seen in sleep-disordered patients.

phenotypes.label: Type 2 diabetes with complications
phenotypes.description: Type 2 diabetes with poor glycemic control and end‑organ complications (neuropathy, retinopathy, diabetic kidney disease) is central, alongside hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and obesity. Coronary atherosclerosis and chronic ischemic heart disease appear frequently, indicating established atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease within a broader cardiometabolic profile.

phenotypes.label: Essential tremor plus colorectal lesions
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between a neurologic tremor cluster (essential tremor, tremor, Parkinson’s disease with associated orthostatic hypotension and dementia) and a colorectal cluster (benign colon neoplasm and residual hemorrhoidal skin tags). These represent unrelated organ systems with no single clinical story tying them together.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: Two unrelated patterns dominate: type 1 diabetes with glycemic complications (hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia, gastroparesis) and prostate malignancy with cancer-related findings (raised PSA, lymphadenopathy, lung nodules). These belong to different organ systems and lack a single unifying clinical mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Lower-extremity venous thromboembolism
phenotypes.description: Records are dominated by lower‑extremity deep venous thrombosis with embolic events. Co-occurring conditions include syncope/loss of consciousness, leg pain, cytopenias, anemia, and autoimmune disease (e.g., SLE), consistent with thrombotic complications and risk factors. Intercurrent infections (vaginitis, sinusitis, urinary tract infection) also appear in the surrounding care context.

phenotypes.label: Psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis
phenotypes.description: Patients with psoriatic disease, combining cutaneous psoriasis and joint involvement consistent with psoriatic arthritis. Associated features include inflammatory dermatoses, nail changes, and hand osteoarthritis patterns, with some records noting fatigue and fibromyalgia.

phenotypes.label: Nail disorders with gait difficulty
phenotypes.description: Patients show nail dystrophy, ingrown nails, onychomycosis, toe pain, and callosities with associated difficulty walking. The pattern reflects podiatric disease limiting ambulation, with mood disorders and hypertension as frequent comorbidities.

phenotypes.label: Herpes zoster (shingles)
phenotypes.description: Encounters centered on uncomplicated herpes zoster (shingles). Typical presentation is an acute dermatomal vesicular eruption with neuropathic pain, usually without documented complications. Other co-listed conditions appear incidental rather than etiologically linked.

phenotypes.label: Acute cardiopulmonary and GI symptoms
phenotypes.description: Presentations are dominated by pain, chest pain, shortness of breath with wheeze/rales, and gastrointestinal symptoms (nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, abdominal pain). Anxiety, bleeding, lymphadenopathy, and paresthesias appear with common comorbidities like hypertension and GERD, consistent with a general acute-symptom pattern rather than a single disease process.

phenotypes.label: Insomnia and hearing loss mix
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between insomnia-spectrum sleep disorders (psychophysiologic insomnia; disorders of initiating and maintaining sleep) and auditory/vestibular problems (bilateral sensorineural hearing loss, unsteadiness, nystagmus). These represent distinct organ systems without a single unifying clinical pathway, so this is not a coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: SLE/Sjögren’s connective tissue disease
phenotypes.description: Systemic lupus erythematosus and Sjögren’s syndrome co-occur with classic connective tissue disease features. Associated findings include inflammatory polyarthropathy, Raynaud’s phenomenon, non-scarring alopecia, leukopenia, interstitial lung disease with dyspnea, and renal involvement with proteinuria. This reflects multi‑organ autoimmunity across musculoskeletal, pulmonary, cutaneous, hematologic, and renal systems.

phenotypes.label: Fatty liver with flushing and dermatitis
phenotypes.description: Patients with nonalcoholic fatty liver disease/steatosis appear alongside vitamin D deficiency and prominent cutaneous findings (seborrheic dermatitis, pruritic rash, alopecia), with frequent reports of flushing. Musculoskeletal degenerative issues (knee effusion, spondylosis) and metabolic comorbidities are common in this group. The pattern reflects a metabolic–inflammatory profile with dermatologic manifestations.

phenotypes.label: Schizoaffective disorder with smoking and obesity
phenotypes.description: Patients with serious mental illness centered on schizoaffective disorder, accompanied by nicotine dependence and severe obesity. Metabolic comorbidities (obesity, hyperlipidemia, prediabetes, hypertension) and smoking-related respiratory symptoms (cough, asthma, obstructive sleep apnea) appear alongside GERD and some chronic hepatitis C. This reflects a tobacco-using SMI population with elevated cardiometabolic and respiratory risk.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine varicella and allergic predisposition with unrelated endocrine (adrenal hypofunction, thyroid nodule, abnormal glucose), neurologic/peripheral nerve, and musculoskeletal problems. This spans disparate systems without a unifying clinical pathway, so it does not represent a single coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Borderline personality disorder
phenotypes.description: Patients are diagnosed with borderline personality disorder as the defining condition. Records frequently include co-documented anxiety, depressive episodes, insomnia, and nonspecific pain or somatic complaints. This reflects behavioral health presentations marked by affective instability and interpersonal dysregulation.
